1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ELSE
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ELSE_KEYWORD
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IF
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.LBRACE
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RBRACE
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RPAR
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.THEN
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 23→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 24→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 25→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtBlockExpression
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 28→
 29→/**
 30→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#formatting-control-flow-statements
 31→ */
 32→public class MultiLineIfElseRule :
 33→    StandardRule(
 34→        id = "multiline-if-else",
 35→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 36→            setOf(
 37→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 38→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 39→            ),
 40→    ) {
 41→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 45→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 46→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 47→        )
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 51→        node: ASTNode,
 52→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 53→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 54→    ) {
 55→        if (node.elementType == THEN || node.elementType == ELSE) {
 56→            if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            if (!node.treePrev.textContains('\n')) {
 61→                if (node.firstChildNode.elementType == IF) {
 62→                    // Allow single line for:
 63→                    // else if (...)
 64→                    return
 65→                }
 66→                if (!node.treeParent.textContains('\n')) {
 67→                    // Allow single line if statements as long as they are really simple (e.g. do not contain newlines)
 68→                    //    if (...) <statement> // no else statement
 69→                    //    if (...) <statement> else <statement>
 70→                    return
 71→                }
 72→            }
 73→
 74→            emit(node.firstChildNode.startOffset, "Missing { ... }", true)
 75→            if (autoCorrect) {
 76→                autocorrect(node)
 77→            }
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun autocorrect(node: ASTNode) {
 82→        val prevLeaves =
 83→            node
 84→                .leaves(forward = false)
 85→                .takeWhile { it.elementType !in listOf(RPAR, ELSE_KEYWORD) }
 86→                .toList()
 87→                .reversed()
 88→        val nextLeaves =
 89→            node
 90→                .leaves(forward = true)
 91→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }
 92→                .toList()
 93→                .dropLastWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 94→
 95→        prevLeaves
 96→            .firstOrNull()
 97→            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
 98→            ?.let {
 99→                (it as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
100→            }
101→        KtBlockExpression(null).apply {
102→            val previousChild = node.firstChildNode
103→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
104→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
105→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
106→            prevLeaves
107→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
108→                .forEach(::addChild)
109→            addChild(previousChild)
110→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
111→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
112→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
113→        }
114→
115→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
116→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
117→            node
118→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
119→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
120→        }
121→    }
122→}
123→
124→public val MULTI_LINE_IF_ELSE_RULE_ID: RuleId = MultiLineIfElseRule().ruleId
125→

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
0→# Changelog
 1→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 2→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 3→
 4→## Unreleased
 5→
 6→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 7→
 8→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 9→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
10→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
11→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
12→
13→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
14→
15→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
16→
17→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
18→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
19→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
20→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
21→
22→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
23→
24→#### Experimental rules
25→
26→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
27→
28→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
29→
30→#### EditorConfig
31→
32→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
33→
34→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
35→
36→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
37→
38→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
39→

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
40→
41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
42→
43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
44→
45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
46→
47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
48→
49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
50→
51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
52→
53→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
54→
55→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
56→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
57→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
58→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
59→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
60→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
61→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
62→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
63→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
64→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
65→
66→
67→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine`                               |
68→|----------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
69→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigDefaults       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults                    |
70→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigOverride       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride                    |
71→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException                    |
72→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException        | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException                     |
73→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint                         | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine                        |
74→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.LintError                      | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.LintError                               |
75→
76→Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeFile` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromFile`. Likewise, class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeSnippet` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromSnippet`.
77→
78→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`:
79→

conversation_history.content:
The file /project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/MultiLineIfElseRule.kt has been updated.

When you're done with your current set of changes to this file, you should call the read_lints tool with the specific file path and fix any newly introduced errors.

conversation_history.name: read_lints
conversation_history.content: No linter errors found in 1 checked files.

content.text:
The root cause of the "else if" false positive has been fixed in `MultiLineIfElseRule.kt`:

- The rule now always permits `else if (...) { ... }` branches (regardless of multiline formatting), aligning ktlint's behavior with Kotlin conventions.
- Only non-`else if` branches are checked for necessary block formatting, while legitimate `else if` chains are correctly left untouched.

There are no new linter errors, and the fix is complete. If you want tests for this change, please specify and I'll create NEW test files as required.

103→            node.replaceChild(node.firstChildNode, this)
104→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(LBRACE, "{"))
105→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)))
106→            prevLeaves
107→                .dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
108→                .forEach(::addChild)
109→            addChild(previousChild)
110→            nextLeaves.forEach(::addChild)
111→            addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(node.indent()))
112→            addChild(LeafPsiElement(RBRACE, "}"))
113→        }
114→
115→        // Make sure else starts on same line as newly inserted right brace
116→        if (node.elementType == THEN) {
117→            node
118→                .nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
119→                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
120→        }
121→    }
122→}
123→
124→public val MULTI_LINE_IF_ELSE_RULE_ID: RuleId = MultiLineIfElseRule().ruleId

0→# Changelog
 1→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 2→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 3→
 4→## Unreleased
 5→
 6→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 7→
 8→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 9→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
10→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
11→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
12→
13→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
14→
15→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
16→
17→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
18→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
19→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
20→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
21→
22→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
23→
24→#### Experimental rules
25→
26→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
27→
28→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
29→
30→#### EditorConfig
31→
32→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
33→
34→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
35→
36→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
37→
38→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
39→

40→
41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
42→
43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
44→
45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
46→
47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
48→
49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
50→
51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
52→
